findings.title: God class detected: Deserializer
findings.description:
Class 'Deserializer' shows signs of being a god class: very high method count (33).

**Role Analysis:** Application — Standard application class

**Metrics:**
- Methods: 33
- Total complexity: 1
- Lines of code: 322

📊 Threshold: 20 (adaptive) | Actual: 33 | Default would be: 20 | Profile: p90=2, p95=3, mean=1.1, n=132
findings.suggested_fix:
Refactor 'Deserializer' to reduce its responsibilities:
**Note:** This class was identified as Application (Standard application class). Thresholds adjusted accordingly.

1. **Extract related methods into separate classes**
- Look for method groups that work with the same data
- Create focused classes with single responsibilities

**Apply SOLID principles:**
- Single Responsibility: Each class should have one reason to change
- Open/Closed: Extend behavior without modifying existing code
- Interface Segregation: Create specific interfaces
- Dependency Inversion: Depend on abstractions
findings.why_it_matters: God classes violate the Single Responsibility Principle. They are difficult to understand, test, and maintain. Changes to one part may unexpectedly affect other parts, leading to bugs and technical debt.

findings.title: unsafe block without SAFETY comment
findings.description: Unsafe blocks should document why they're safe. Add a `// SAFETY:` comment.
findings.why_it_matters: Unsafe code bypasses Rust's safety guarantees. SAFETY comments are essential for code review.

findings.title: Mutual recursion: 11 functions in call cycle (complexity 64)
findings.description: Mutual recursion detected: 11 functions form a call cycle. Cycle: /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/serde/serde_derive/src/internals/receiver.rs::impl<ReplaceReceiver<'_>>::visit_expr_mut:264 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/serde/serde_derive/src/internals/receiver.rs::impl<ReplaceReceiver<'_>>::visit_expr_path_mut:98 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/serde/serde_derive/src/internals/receiver.rs::impl<ReplaceReceiver<'_>>::visit_expr_path_mut_impl:161 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/serde/serde_derive/src/internals/receiver.rs::impl<ReplaceReceiver<'_>>::visit_path_arguments_mut:174 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/serde/serde_derive/src/internals/receiver.rs::impl<ReplaceReceiver<'_>>::visit_path_mut:168 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/serde/serde_derive/src/internals/receiver.rs::impl<ReplaceReceiver<'_>>::visit_return_type_mut:200 ... (+5 more). Aggregate complexity: 64.
findings.suggested_fix: Break the cycle by extracting shared logic into a common helper, introducing an event system, or restructuring the call chain to be unidirectional.
findings.why_it_matters: Mutual recursion creates tight coupling between functions, making them impossible to understand, test, or refactor independently. It can also cause stack overflows if the recursion depth is unbounded.

findings.title: Panic risk: unwrap() without context
findings.description: Using `unwrap()` can cause panics. Consider `?`, `unwrap_or`, or proper error handling.
findings.why_it_matters: Panics crash the program without recovery. Using proper error handling makes code more robust and debuggable.

findings.title: High panic density in `main`: 4 panic-family calls
findings.description: [COMPOUND: 2 co-located issues] Function `main` contains 4 calls to `.unwrap()`, `.expect()`, or `panic!()` at line 21, line 22, line 24, line 44. This makes the function fragile and likely to panic at runtime.
findings.suggested_fix:
Reduce panic points by:
- Using `?` operator for error propagation
- Using `unwrap_or` / `unwrap_or_default` / `unwrap_or_else`
- Returning `Result<T, E>` instead of panicking
- Extracting fallible logic into a helper that returns Result
findings.why_it_matters: Functions with many panic points are fragile. A single unexpected None/Err will crash the program. Consolidating error handling makes code more robust and easier to reason about.

findings.title: Structural bridge: `/tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/serde/serde_core/src/de/value.rs::impl<IntoDeserializer<'de, E> for ()>::into_deserializer:131` separates components of [740, 26]
findings.description: `/tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/serde/serde_core/src/de/value.rs::impl<IntoDeserializer<'de, E> for ()>::into_deserializer:131` is a structural bridge. Removing it would split the graph into components of sizes [740, 26]. All communication between these components currently flows through this single node.
findings.suggested_fix: Reduce coupling through this node by introducing alternative call paths, extracting shared interfaces, or splitting the bridge function into independently accessible units.
findings.why_it_matters: A structural bridge is a single node whose failure disconnects entire subsystems. This creates fragile architecture where a bug, API change, or refactor in one function can cascade to all dependent components.

findings.title: Long method: deserialize (305 lines)
findings.description:
Function 'deserialize' has 305 lines (effective threshold: 150).

**Graph Analysis:**
Orchestrator pattern: calls 12 functions (reduced severity)
Calls 5 different modules - possible split points
findings.suggested_fix:
This appears to be an orchestrator function (coordinates many calls).
If it must remain long, ensure it:
1. Has clear section comments
2. Handles errors at each step
3. Has a clear flow (consider a state machine for complex flows)
findings.why_it_matters: Long methods are hard to understand, test, and maintain. Each function should do one thing well.

findings.title: Excessive nesting: 9 levels in from_ast
findings.description:
9 levels of nesting (threshold: 7).

**Analysis:**
In function: `from_ast`
High complexity: 111 (nesting compounds this)
Contains 2 match/switch level(s) (discounted from raw depth 11)
Existing helpers that could reduce nesting: decide_identifier, decide_tag, parse_lit_into_path

Threshold: 5 (adaptive) | Actual: 9 | Default would be: 5 | Profile: p90=3, p95=4, mean=0.8, n=862
findings.suggested_fix:
This function already has helpers like `decide_identifier`. Consider:
1. Extract more nested blocks into similar helpers
2. Use guard clauses (early returns) to reduce nesting
3. Replace nested ifs with switch/match
findings.why_it_matters: Deep nesting makes code hard to read and maintain. Each level increases cognitive load exponentially.
